title.zh: 細胞營養偵測器的60奈米彎月形密碼：KICSTOR-GATOR1-SAMTOR複合體揭開mTORC1調控之謎
title.en: The Lysosome's 60-Nanometer Crescent: How KICSTOR-GATOR1-SAMTOR Decodes Nutrient Signals

summary.zh: 【總結】這項研究揭開細胞營養感知機制的核心密碼：科學家首次解析出位於溶酶體表面的KICSTOR-GATOR1-SAMTOR營養感應超複合體結構，呈現出60奈米長的彎月形態。這個「分子月牙」如同精密的營養開關站，當氨基酸匱乏時，能精準調控mTORC1——細胞生長的master regulator。研究發現SAMTOR（甲硫氨酸感應器）以獨特方式結合KICSTOR，卻無法同時結合甲硫氨酸，揭開細胞如何將甲硫氨酸濃度與生長調控連結的機制。更驚人的是，KICSTOR與GATOR1形成二聚體超複合體，強制GATOR1保持特定方位，只允許低親和力的 Rag GTPase 結合，確保細胞在營養短缺時不會錯誤激活生長。
summary.en: **Summary:** Scientists have cracked the molecular code of nutrient sensing at the lysosome, revealing how a 60-nanometer crescent-shaped supercomplex controls the master growth regulator mTORC1. Using cryo-EM, researchers uncovered the precise 3D structure of the KICSTOR-GATOR1-SAMTOR complex—the cell's nutrient detection system. This molecular crescent acts as a precision docking station: when amino acids are scarce, it ensures GATOR1 (the "off switch") properly positions itself to deactivate mTORC1. The breakthrough also explains how SAMTOR, the methionine sensor, binds KICSTOR in a way that blocks S-adenosylmethionine binding—a clever evolutionary trick linking methionine levels to growth control. Most importantly, the dimeric supercomplex forces GATOR1 into a specific orientation that only permits "low-affinity" Rag GTPase binding, ensuring cells don't accidentally activate growth during starvation.

keyArticle.title: Structure of the lysosomal KICSTOR-GATOR1-SAMTOR nutrient-sensing supercomplex
